This stage is the startup-time policy assembler that turns many independent inputs into the single effective runtime configuration the rest of the system obeys. It sits after raw file/source discovery and before session creation, servers, tools, and UI begin using settings.

Its two main sub-stages do most of the heavy lifting: configuration ingestion merges managed, cloud, user, project, thread, and CLI layers into validated config and requirements with provenance; feature and asset resolution then decides feature flags, plugin/provider catalogs, MCP declarations, bundled skills, model metadata, presets, and other built-in resources that must exist before runtime surfaces come up.

The directly assigned files refine and expose that result. Shared CLI option merging, app-server config loading, project-root markers, runtime executable paths, and the core resolved-config model define precedence and shape. Permission TOML parsing, canonical permission-profile compilation, resolved-profile identity, Windows-specific sandbox translation, capability/ACL path resolution, and sandbox-mode setup convert user intent into enforceable filesystem and network policy. Tool-config and service-tier/keymap/TUI settings project startup policy into concrete execution and UI behavior, while lockfile export, debug-config output, editable marketplace/MCP/plugin config, migration utilities, and persistence glue let callers inspect, migrate, and safely update the assembled configuration.
